model_summary: INITIAL gVAR-compatible observation model with 6 criterion variables representing nightmare-related sleep disruption and 6 predictor variables spanning cognitive-behavioral sleep strategies and physiological/environmental supports. Design emphasizes EMA-based momentary measures for criteria and a mix of EMA and passive/sensor-informed predictors. Total nodes = 12. Dense P->C adjacency fully enumerated; sparse P->P and C->C edges included for sparsity constraints.
variable_selection_notes: Case focuses on nightmare frequency/intensity, anticipatory fear of sleep, autonomic arousal on awakening, post-awakening activation, and difficulty resuming sleep. Predictor set covers sleep routines, sleep environment, presleep downshift, breathing/relaxation, CBT sleep skills, and imagery rescripting. All variables are non-pharmacological and suitable for digital data capture.

momentary_ema.notes: Momentary assessments for all six criteria during daytime and evening prompts; align with sleep-wake cycle.

daily_diary.notes: Evening diary capturing perceived sleep quality and nightmare-related stress.

passive_streams.notes: No passive streams enabled in initial iteration.

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: Initial readiness moderate; ensure high compliance with EMA prompts.
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: Expect high within-person variability in nightmare frequency and arousal; plan for detrending if needed.
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: Implement reminder prompts and fallback diary entries to mitigate missing data.

safety_notes: This is not a diagnosis or treatment recommendation. If distress worsens or there are safety concerns (e.g., suicidality, intensified nightmares with self-harm risk), seek immediate professional help. Provide crisis resources and escalation pathways in the app.

criteria_variables.label: Nightmare-triggered awakenings (occurrence)

measurement.notes: Prompt user to indicate number of nightmare-triggered awakenings since last prompt; scale reflects frequency.

criteria_variables.mapped_leaf_embed_path: CLINICAL / DSM-5TR / sleep-wake disorders / medication or substance-induced sleep-wake disorders / medication or substance-induced parasomnias / medication-associated nightmare disorder / *nocturnal_awakenings_due_to_nightmares
criteria_variables.mapped_rationale: Variable label Nightmare-triggered awakenings (occurrence) maps best to the leaf nocturnal_awakenings_due_to_nightmares (idx 25298). This criterion captures awakenings that occur at night and are caused by nightmares, aligning with the EMA_self_report event-count measurement (C01_event) collected via mobile app as a momentary signal (4 prompts per day, recall since last prompt). The mechanism involves nightmare-induced autonomic arousal leading to nocturnal awakening, fitting the Sleep-Wake disorders / nightmare disorder leaf and representing a precise, event-based frequency measure for non-pharmacological sleep disruption.

criteria_variables.label: Nightmare vividness/intensity

measurement.notes: Capture subjective vividness of dream content per awakening.

criteria_variables.mapped_leaf_full_path: CLINICAL / DSM-5TR / substance-related and addictive disorders / substance-induced disorders / substance withdrawal / stimulant withdrawal / amphetamine or methamphetamine withdrawal / vivid_dreams
criteria_variables.mapped_leaf_embed_path: CLINICAL / DSM-5TR / substance-related and addictive disorders / substance-induced disorders / substance withdrawal / stimulant withdrawal / amphetamine or methamphetamine withdrawal / *vivid_dreams
criteria_variables.mapped_rationale: C02 is Nightmare vividness/intensity measured via EMA self-report on a 0-9 scale. The closest leaf that directly encodes dream vividness/intensity is vivid_dreams (idx 22671). Although that leaf sits under a withdrawal-related subtree, its label explicitly references dream vividness, aligning with the intended operational construct of subjective dream/nightmare vividness. The measurement mode (EMA_self_report on mobile app) and the intent to capture momentary subjective experience support mapping to this leaf over other Nightmare- or dream-content leaves that focus on distress, recall, or frequency rather than vividness per se.
criteria_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Nightmare vividness/intensity | Operational item/signal: C02_intensity | Polarity: higher_is_better | Notes: Capture subjective vividness of dream content per awakening.

measurement.notes: Assess fear/dread before attempting sleep.

The most specific matching criterion leaf is bedtime_anxiety_present, which directly captures bedtime anxiety in insomnia-related contexts and aligns with EMA self-report measurements (4 prompts/day, 0-9 scale) for momentary assessment of pre-sleep fear.

criteria_variables.label: Acute autonomic arousal on awakening

measurement.notes: Subjective arousal indicators (sweating, heart rate) at awakening.

criteria_variables.mapped_leaf_embed_path: CLINICAL / DSM-5TR / sleep-wake disorders / insomnia disorders / chronic insomnia disorder / early-morning awakening insomnia / *morning_hyperarousal_signs_post_awakening
criteria_variables.mapped_rationale: Variable C04 = Acute autonomic arousal on awakening; measurement EMA self-report; criterion path Sleep_Disruption/Autonomic_Arousal_Awakening. The closest, most specific operational leaf is morning_hyperarousal_sign_post_awakening (post-awakening autonomic arousal signs), which captures autonomic arousal after awakening and aligns with morning hyperarousal concepts. It uses post-awakening signs such as sweating or tachycardia; matches measurement mode (momentary self-report) via EMA in mobile app. Other leaves concern nocturnal episodes or arousal indexes not tied to awakening.

criteria_variables.label: Prolonged post-awakening physiological/psychological activation

measurement.notes: Time-to-calm-down after nightmare; perceived persistence.

criteria_variables.mapped_leaf_full_path: CLINICAL / DSM-5TR / sleep-wake disorders / insomnia disorders / chronic insomnia disorder / early-morning awakening insomnia / morning_hyperarousal_signs_post_awakening
criteria_variables.mapped_leaf_embed_path: CLINICAL / DSM-5TR / sleep-wake disorders / insomnia disorders / chronic insomnia disorder / early-morning awakening insomnia / *morning_hyperarousal_signs_post_awakening
criteria_variables.mapped_rationale: Best match is morning_hyperarousal_signs_post_awakening (idx 26345) for C05_activation, which targets post-awakening physiological arousal and morning activation after nightmares. It aligns with the variable label (prolonged post-awakening activation) and the criterion path Sleep_Disruption/Post_Awakening_Activation. The measurement is EMA self-report via mobile app with momentary sampling and the C05_activation signal, matching the intended real-time activation construct. Other leaves either focus on nightmare during sleep (elevated autonomic arousal during nightmare, awakening due to nightmare) or cognitive/post-awakening processes (intrusive rumination), but do not capture the combined post-awakening hyperarousal activation this variable describes.

The best-match leaf is nightmare_induced_sleep_fragmentation, which explicitly encodes sleep fragmentation and post-awakening sleep maintenance difficulty caused by nightmares, aligning with the intended operational meaning of C06_resumption. The candidate is the most specific nightmare-related criterion that captures post-awakening resumption difficulty rather than general awakenings or subjective sleep quality.

predictor_variables.label: Sleep Routine/CBT-like bedtime structuring
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Structured routines reduce sleep-associated conditioned arousal and improve sleep onset.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Imagery rehearsal, fixed wind-down, consistent bed time.

measurement.notes: assesses adherence to bedtime routine and perceived effectiveness.

predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: Best match is leaf 3822: presleep_detachment_routine_design_psychological. The variable describes a CBT-like bedtime structuring and sleep routine with an EMA self-report measure of routine quality (P01_routine_quality). The leaf is explicitly in the PSYCHO domain and captures presleep routine design with psychological detachment, aligning with the intended Sleep Routine/CBT-like bedtime structuring. It is the most specific operational construct among candidates (more specific than general behavioral_routine_design or presleep_downshift). Mechanism rationale: structured routines reduce sleep-related conditioned arousal and rumination at onset, improving ease of sleep initiation.
predictor_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Sleep Routine/CBT-like bedtime structuring | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Structured routines reduce sleep-associated conditioned arousal and improve sleep onset. | Intervention examples: Imagery rehearsal, fixed wind-down, consistent bed time. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | P01_routine_quality | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / sleep_related_behaviors / sleep_routines

predictor_variables.label: Sleep Environment Optimization (sleep-conducive room factors)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Air quality, temperature, light, and noise influence sleep onset and nocturnal arousal.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Optimize lighting, reduce noise, cool dark room.

measurement.notes: Aggregated daily environment quality score.

predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: Best match for P02 Sleep Environment Optimization is the ventilation_improvement leaf under BIO / sleep_circadian_and_restoration / sleep_environment / air_allergen_and_irritant_control. It targets air quality/ventilation in the sleep space, aligning with the mechanism that air quality, temperature, light, and noise influence sleep onset and nocturnal arousal. The measurement item P02_environment_index represents an aggregated daily assessment of bedroom environmental quality, consistent with a daily ventilation/air-quality improvement focus. This leaf is the most specific environmental-optimization construct among candidates and sits within the BIO Sleep_Environment domain.
predictor_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Sleep Environment Optimization (sleep-conducive room factors) | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Air quality, temperature, light, and noise influence sleep onset and nocturnal arousal. | Intervention examples: Optimize lighting, reduce noise, cool dark room. | Measurement: passive_phone_sensor | P02_environment_index | Prior ontology hint: BIO / Sleep_Environment

predictor_variables.label: Presleep Downshift/Rituals (downshift from wakefulness to sleep)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Relaxation and mental unloading reduce pre-sleep arousal and nightmare anticipation.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Breathing, progressive muscle relaxation, sensory downshift.

measurement.notes: Assess perceived effectiveness of presleep downshift.

expected_effects.rationale: Better presleep routine reduces anticipatory fear.

predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: Variable maps to Presleep Downshift/Rituals (downshift from wakefulness to sleep) with a PSYCHO domain intent. Among candidates that implement presleep_downshift, breathwork_relaxation is the most direct, operation-level downshift tactic targeting relaxation to reduce pre-sleep arousal and nightmare anticipation. It provides a concrete EMA-self-reportable predictor (P03_downshift_effort) and aligns with the mechanism (relaxation and mental unloading). While other presleep_downshift options (e.g., guided_meditation, progressive_muscle_relaxation, body_scan) are plausible, breathwork_relaxation offers the most specific, momentary downshift ritual that matches the intended construct and measurement approach in this pseudoprofile context.
predictor_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Presleep Downshift/Rituals (downshift from wakefulness to sleep) | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Relaxation and mental unloading reduce pre-sleep arousal and nightmare anticipation. | Intervention examples: Breathing, progressive muscle relaxation, sensory downshift. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | P03_downshift_effort | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / presleep_downshift

predictor_variables.label: Breathing/Detachment during pre-sleep for arousal downshift
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Slow breathing and detachment reduce cognitive arousal before sleep.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: paced breathing, body scan, cognitive detachment.

measurement.notes: Capture perceived effectiveness of calming breathing sequences.

predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: The variable targets presleep detachment as a mechanism to downshift arousal. Among candidates, idx 3822 maps exactly to a PSYCHO leaf focused on cognitive-behavioral sleep interventions with presleep detachment routines (presleep_detachment_routine_design_psychological). This is the most specific operational construct aligning with the 'Detachment during pre-sleep' component of the variable. It captures the intended mechanism (cognitive detachment to reduce arousal) within a CBT framework suitable for EMA-based assessment. While breathing is also mentioned, the closest leaf that directly encodes the presleep detachment routine is the detachment design leaf. Measurement modality (EMA_self_report via mobile) is compatible with the pseudoprofile context.
predictor_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Breathing/Detachment during pre-sleep for arousal downshift | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Slow breathing and detachment reduce cognitive arousal before sleep. | Intervention examples: paced breathing, body scan, cognitive detachment. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | P04_breathing_effort | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / presleep_breathing_detachment

predictor_variables.label: Sleep-related CBT skills training / sleep appraisals
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Cognitive restructuring and behavioral experiments reduce sleep-related worry and nightmare threat beliefs.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: CBT-I components: stimulus control, cognitive restructuring, sleep restriction.

measurement.notes: Assess engagement and perceived efficacy of CBT sleep strategies.

expected_effects.rationale: Enhanced sleep preparation and reduced sleep anxiety.

predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: Best-matching leaf: PSYCHO / cognitive_behavioral_therapies / sleep_related_cognitive_behavioral_interventions / cognitive_work / sleep_appraisals / cognitive_reframe_sleep_threat_appraisals. It is the most specific operational construct for 'Sleep-related CBT skills training / sleep appraisals', focusing on cognitive reframing of sleep-threat beliefs within the sleep-appraisal cognitive work domain. The variable P05 corresponds to CBT-I practice (P05_cbti_practice) measured via EMA_self_report on a mobile app, aligning with the mechanism that cognitive restructuring reduces nightmare-related sleep worry. Other leaves cover broader CBT-I components or non-sleep contexts; none are as specifically tailored to sleep-threat appraisal reframing as this one.
predictor_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Sleep-related CBT skills training / sleep appraisals | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Cognitive restructuring and behavioral experiments reduce sleep-related worry and nightmare threat beliefs. | Intervention examples: CBT-I components: stimulus control, cognitive restructuring, sleep restriction. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | P05_cbti_practice | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / CBT_sleep_skills

predictor_variables.label: Nightmare imagery rehearsal / rescripting techniques
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Imagery rehearsal reduces nightmare frequency/intensity through cognitive rehearsal and mastery.

measurement.notes: Assess frequency and perceived efficacy of imagery rehearsal.

expected_effects.rationale: Aids in reducing nightmare vividness via rehearsal exposure.

predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: Best match: variable label 'Nightmare imagery rehearsal / rescripting techniques' aligns with leaf 'nightmare_imagery_rehearsal' under PSYCHO / trauma_memory_imagery_and_narrative_work / imagery_and_rescripting_interventions. Measurement mode EMA_self_report via mobile app (P06_imagery_practice) corresponds to an momentary, non-pharmacological imagery rehearsal intervention. Mechanism rationale: imagery rehearsal reduces nightmare frequency/intensity via cognitive rehearsal and mastery; this leaf directly captures this intervention category.
predictor_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Nightmare imagery rehearsal / rescripting techniques | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Imagery rehearsal reduces nightmare frequency/intensity through cognitive rehearsal and mastery. | Intervention examples: rescripting, imagined outcomes, dream rewriting. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | P06_imagery_practice | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / nightmare_imagery_rehearsal

expected_collinearity_risks: Possible collinearity between C01 and C02 when reporting frequent awakenings and high intensity
Overlap between C04 and C05 arousal items if not time-anchored
expected_floor_ceiling_risks: C03/C05 may saturate at high values if anticipatory fear dominates
variance_enhancement_suggestions: Include alternating day-of-week prompts to capture weekly variation
excluded_or_deprioritized_candidates: No pharmacological predictors included in initial model
diagnostics.gvar_feasibility_summary: Feasible with 12 nodes; EMA-based criterion measures and a mix of predictor modalities supported.

measurement.notes: Prompt user to indicate number of nightmare-triggered awakenings since last prompt; scale reflects frequency.

criteria.label: Nightmare vividness/intensity
criteria.query_text_used: Nightmare vividness/intensity | Operational item/signal: C02_intensity | Polarity: higher_is_better | Notes: Capture subjective vividness of dream content per awakening.

mapping.rationale: C02 is Nightmare vividness/intensity measured via EMA self-report on a 0-9 scale. The closest leaf that directly encodes dream vividness/intensity is vivid_dreams (idx 22671). Although that leaf sits under a withdrawal-related subtree, its label explicitly references dream vividness, aligning with the intended operational construct of subjective dream/nightmare vividness. The measurement mode (EMA_self_report on mobile app) and the intent to capture momentary subjective experience support mapping to this leaf over other Nightmare- or dream-content leaves that focus on distress, recall, or frequency rather than vividness per se.

criteria.label: Anticipatory fear of sleep / reluctance to go to bed
criteria.query_text_used: Anticipatory fear of sleep / reluctance to go to bed | Operational item/signal: C03_thoughts | Polarity: higher_is_better | Notes: Assess fear/dread before attempting sleep.

mapping.rationale: The variable represents anticipatory fear before attempting sleep (reluctance to go to bed). The most specific matching criterion leaf is bedtime_anxiety_present, which directly captures bedtime anxiety in insomnia-related contexts and aligns with EMA self-report measurements (4 prompts/day, 0-9 scale) for momentary assessment of pre-sleep fear.

source_variable.label: Anticipatory fear of sleep / reluctance to go to bed

measurement.notes: Assess fear/dread before attempting sleep.

criteria.label: Acute autonomic arousal on awakening
criteria.query_text_used: Acute autonomic arousal on awakening | Operational item/signal: C04_arousal | Polarity: higher_is_better | Notes: Subjective arousal indicators (sweating, heart rate) at awakening.

mapping.rationale: Variable C04 = Acute autonomic arousal on awakening; measurement EMA self-report; criterion path Sleep_Disruption/Autonomic_Arousal_Awakening. The closest, most specific operational leaf is morning_hyperarousal_sign_post_awakening (post-awakening autonomic arousal signs), which captures autonomic arousal after awakening and aligns with morning hyperarousal concepts. It uses post-awakening signs such as sweating or tachycardia; matches measurement mode (momentary self-report) via EMA in mobile app. Other leaves concern nocturnal episodes or arousal indexes not tied to awakening.

criteria.label: Prolonged post-awakening physiological/psychological activation
criteria.query_text_used: Prolonged post-awakening physiological/psychological activation | Operational item/signal: C05_activation | Polarity: higher_is_better | Notes: Time-to-calm-down after nightmare; perceived persistence.

mapping.rationale: Best match is morning_hyperarousal_signs_post_awakening (idx 26345) for C05_activation, which targets post-awakening physiological arousal and morning activation after nightmares. It aligns with the variable label (prolonged post-awakening activation) and the criterion path Sleep_Disruption/Post_Awakening_Activation. The measurement is EMA self-report via mobile app with momentary sampling and the C05_activation signal, matching the intended real-time activation construct. Other leaves either focus on nightmare during sleep (elevated autonomic arousal during nightmare, awakening due to nightmare) or cognitive/post-awakening processes (intrusive rumination), but do not capture the combined post-awakening hyperarousal activation this variable describes.

measurement.notes: Time-to-calm-down after nightmare; perceived persistence.

criteria.label: Difficulty resuming sleep after nightmare awakenings
criteria.query_text_used: Difficulty resuming sleep after nightmare awakenings | Operational item/signal: C06_resumption | Polarity: higher_is_better | Notes: Self-reported difficulty returning to sleep after awakenings.

mapping.rationale: Variable label: Difficulty resuming sleep after nightmare awakenings (C06). Measurement item: C06_resumption, EMA_self_report on mobile app (momentary, 0-9). The best-match leaf is nightmare_induced_sleep_fragmentation, which explicitly encodes sleep fragmentation and post-awakening sleep maintenance difficulty caused by nightmares, aligning with the intended operational meaning of C06_resumption. The candidate is the most specific nightmare-related criterion that captures post-awakening resumption difficulty rather than general awakenings or subjective sleep quality.

source_variable.label: Difficulty resuming sleep after nightmare awakenings

measurement.notes: Self-reported difficulty returning to sleep after awakenings.

predictors.label: Sleep Routine/CBT-like bedtime structuring
predictors.query_text_used: Sleep Routine/CBT-like bedtime structuring | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Structured routines reduce sleep-associated conditioned arousal and improve sleep onset. | Intervention examples: Imagery rehearsal, fixed wind-down, consistent bed time. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | P01_routine_quality | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / sleep_related_behaviors / sleep_routines

mapping.rationale: Best match is leaf 3822: presleep_detachment_routine_design_psychological. The variable describes a CBT-like bedtime structuring and sleep routine with an EMA self-report measure of routine quality (P01_routine_quality). The leaf is explicitly in the PSYCHO domain and captures presleep routine design with psychological detachment, aligning with the intended Sleep Routine/CBT-like bedtime structuring. It is the most specific operational construct among candidates (more specific than general behavioral_routine_design or presleep_downshift). Mechanism rationale: structured routines reduce sleep-related conditioned arousal and rumination at onset, improving ease of sleep initiation.

source_variable.label: Sleep Routine/CBT-like bedtime structuring
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Structured routines reduce sleep-associated conditioned arousal and improve sleep onset.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Imagery rehearsal, fixed wind-down, consistent bed time.

measurement.notes: assesses adherence to bedtime routine and perceived effectiveness.

predictors.label: Sleep Environment Optimization (sleep-conducive room factors)
predictors.query_text_used: Sleep Environment Optimization (sleep-conducive room factors) | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Air quality, temperature, light, and noise influence sleep onset and nocturnal arousal. | Intervention examples: Optimize lighting, reduce noise, cool dark room. | Measurement: passive_phone_sensor | P02_environment_index | Prior ontology hint: BIO / Sleep_Environment

mapping.rationale: Best match for P02 Sleep Environment Optimization is the ventilation_improvement leaf under BIO / sleep_circadian_and_restoration / sleep_environment / air_allergen_and_irritant_control. It targets air quality/ventilation in the sleep space, aligning with the mechanism that air quality, temperature, light, and noise influence sleep onset and nocturnal arousal. The measurement item P02_environment_index represents an aggregated daily assessment of bedroom environmental quality, consistent with a daily ventilation/air-quality improvement focus. This leaf is the most specific environmental-optimization construct among candidates and sits within the BIO Sleep_Environment domain.

source_variable.label: Sleep Environment Optimization (sleep-conducive room factors)
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Air quality, temperature, light, and noise influence sleep onset and nocturnal arousal.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Optimize lighting, reduce noise, cool dark room.

measurement.notes: Aggregated daily environment quality score.

predictors.label: Presleep Downshift/Rituals (downshift from wakefulness to sleep)
predictors.query_text_used: Presleep Downshift/Rituals (downshift from wakefulness to sleep) | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Relaxation and mental unloading reduce pre-sleep arousal and nightmare anticipation. | Intervention examples: Breathing, progressive muscle relaxation, sensory downshift. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | P03_downshift_effort | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / presleep_downshift

mapping.rationale: Variable maps to Presleep Downshift/Rituals (downshift from wakefulness to sleep) with a PSYCHO domain intent. Among candidates that implement presleep_downshift, breathwork_relaxation is the most direct, operation-level downshift tactic targeting relaxation to reduce pre-sleep arousal and nightmare anticipation. It provides a concrete EMA-self-reportable predictor (P03_downshift_effort) and aligns with the mechanism (relaxation and mental unloading). While other presleep_downshift options (e.g., guided_meditation, progressive_muscle_relaxation, body_scan) are plausible, breathwork_relaxation offers the most specific, momentary downshift ritual that matches the intended construct and measurement approach in this pseudoprofile context.

source_variable.label: Presleep Downshift/Rituals (downshift from wakefulness to sleep)
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Relaxation and mental unloading reduce pre-sleep arousal and nightmare anticipation.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Breathing, progressive muscle relaxation, sensory downshift.

measurement.notes: Assess perceived effectiveness of presleep downshift.

expected_effects.rationale: Better presleep routine reduces anticipatory fear.

predictors.label: Breathing/Detachment during pre-sleep for arousal downshift
predictors.query_text_used: Breathing/Detachment during pre-sleep for arousal downshift | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Slow breathing and detachment reduce cognitive arousal before sleep. | Intervention examples: paced breathing, body scan, cognitive detachment. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | P04_breathing_effort | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / presleep_breathing_detachment

mapping.rationale: The variable targets presleep detachment as a mechanism to downshift arousal. Among candidates, idx 3822 maps exactly to a PSYCHO leaf focused on cognitive-behavioral sleep interventions with presleep detachment routines (presleep_detachment_routine_design_psychological). This is the most specific operational construct aligning with the 'Detachment during pre-sleep' component of the variable. It captures the intended mechanism (cognitive detachment to reduce arousal) within a CBT framework suitable for EMA-based assessment. While breathing is also mentioned, the closest leaf that directly encodes the presleep detachment routine is the detachment design leaf. Measurement modality (EMA_self_report via mobile) is compatible with the pseudoprofile context.

source_variable.label: Breathing/Detachment during pre-sleep for arousal downshift
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Slow breathing and detachment reduce cognitive arousal before sleep.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: paced breathing, body scan, cognitive detachment.

measurement.notes: Capture perceived effectiveness of calming breathing sequences.

predictors.label: Sleep-related CBT skills training / sleep appraisals
predictors.query_text_used: Sleep-related CBT skills training / sleep appraisals | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Cognitive restructuring and behavioral experiments reduce sleep-related worry and nightmare threat beliefs. | Intervention examples: CBT-I components: stimulus control, cognitive restructuring, sleep restriction. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | P05_cbti_practice | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / CBT_sleep_skills

mapping.rationale: Best-matching leaf: PSYCHO / cognitive_behavioral_therapies / sleep_related_cognitive_behavioral_interventions / cognitive_work / sleep_appraisals / cognitive_reframe_sleep_threat_appraisals. It is the most specific operational construct for 'Sleep-related CBT skills training / sleep appraisals', focusing on cognitive reframing of sleep-threat beliefs within the sleep-appraisal cognitive work domain. The variable P05 corresponds to CBT-I practice (P05_cbti_practice) measured via EMA_self_report on a mobile app, aligning with the mechanism that cognitive restructuring reduces nightmare-related sleep worry. Other leaves cover broader CBT-I components or non-sleep contexts; none are as specifically tailored to sleep-threat appraisal reframing as this one.

source_variable.label: Nightmare imagery rehearsal / rescripting techniques

expected_effects.rationale: Aids in reducing nightmare vividness via rehearsal exposure.
